title: sertula infesta
intro: Sertula infesta, also known as the Cocklebur, is a biennial or annual plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sertula infesta include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. The herbal preparations of Sertula infesta include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
